project: Hal Cloud Daytona Sandbox Orchestration v1
description: Enable terminal-first cloud orchestration for hal workloads in Daytona sandboxes with durable state, idempotent side effects, and deterministic .hal bundle upload/restore.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create runs table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want durable run records so orchestration can recover after restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a runs table with columns id, repo, base_branch, engine, auth_profile_id, scope_ref, status, attempt_count, max_attempts, deadline_at, input_snapshot_id, latest_snapshot_id, latest_snapshot_version, created_at, and updated_at
Restrict runs.status to exactly queued, claimed, running, retrying, succeeded, failed, or canceled
Create a queue index on (status, created_at)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create attempts table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want attempt-level state so heartbeat and retry behavior is explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an attempts table with columns id, run_id, attempt_number, worker_id, sandbox_id, status, started_at, heartbeat_at, lease_expires_at, ended_at, error_code, and error_message
Enforce at most one active attempt per run_id
Create indexes on (run_id), (status), and (lease_expires_at)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create events table
userStories.description: As an operator, I want durable run timelines so status and logs commands can read authoritative history.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an events table with columns id, run_id, attempt_id, event_type, payload_json, redacted, and created_at
Ensure submit, worker, and reconciler paths append new event rows and never update or delete existing rows
Create an index on (run_id, created_at)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create idempotency keys table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want persisted idempotency keys so retries cannot duplicate external side effects.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an idempotency_keys table with columns key, run_id, side_effect_type, result_ref, and created_at
Enforce a unique constraint on idempotency_keys.key
Return a deterministic duplicate_key domain error on duplicate insert attempts
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create auth profiles table
userStories.description: As an operator, I want reusable auth profiles so credentials are linked once and reused by workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an auth_profiles table with columns id, owner_id, provider, mode, secret_ref, status, max_concurrent_runs, runtime_metadata_json, last_validated_at, expires_at, last_error_code, version, created_at, and updated_at
Restrict auth_profiles.status to exactly pending_link, linked, invalid, or revoked
Enforce max_concurrent_runs >= 1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create auth profile locks table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want lease-based locks so shared subscription sessions are concurrency-safe.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an auth_profile_locks table with columns auth_profile_id, run_id, worker_id, acquired_at, heartbeat_at, lease_expires_at, and released_at
Create an index on (auth_profile_id, lease_expires_at)
Add a constraint preventing duplicate active lock rows for the same (auth_profile_id, run_id)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create run state snapshots table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want durable .hal state snapshots so retries can restore run context after sandbox loss.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a run_state_snapshots table with columns id, run_id, attempt_id, snapshot_kind, version, sha256, size_bytes, content_encoding, content_blob, and created_at
Enforce a unique constraint on (run_id, version)
Create an index on (run_id, created_at)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Define Store interface and domain errors
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a single persistence contract so orchestration logic is adapter-agnostic.
acceptanceCriteria: Define Store methods for enqueue, claim, transitions, attempt heartbeats, events, idempotency, auth profile read and update, lock acquire and renew and release, and snapshot put and get and listLatest
Define shared domain errors not_found, conflict, duplicate_key, lease_expired, and invalid_transition
Compile service-layer orchestration code without adapter-specific imports
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Add adapter contract test suite skeleton
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want adapter-neutral tests so correctness rules are defined once.
acceptanceCriteria: Add contract tests covering enqueue, atomic claim, one-active-attempt invariant, heartbeat renew, and transition guards
Add a parallel claim test that asserts exactly one winner per run
Support adapter factory injection via newStoreForTest
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Postgres enqueue and claim operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Postgres run-claim primitives so workers can safely pick work.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Postgres store methods for run enqueue and atomic claim
Ensure claim SQL is concurrency-safe under parallel workers
Add focused tests for enqueue plus one-winner claim behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Postgres auth lock lease operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Postgres lock primitives so auth profile concurrency is enforceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Postgres methods for auth lock acquire, renew, and release
Enforce lease-expiry semantics on renew operations
Add focused tests for lock-acquisition conflict and stale-lock reclaim paths
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Turso run-claim primitives so workers can safely pick work.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Turso store methods for run enqueue and atomic claim
Match one-winner claim semantics under contention
Add focused tests for enqueue plus one-winner claim behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Turso auth lock lease operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Turso lock primitives so auth profile concurrency is enforceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Turso methods for auth lock acquire, renew, and release
Enforce lease-expiry semantics on renew operations
Add focused tests for lock-acquisition conflict and stale-lock reclaim paths
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Run parity benchmark harness for both adapters
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I want comparable adapter metrics so default DB choice is evidence-based.
acceptanceCriteria: Execute benchmark scenarios claim_contention and auth_lock_contention for both adapters
Emit JSON rows with keys scenario, adapter, runs, errors, claim_p95_ms, heartbeat_p95_ms, duplicate_claims, and lock_overcommit_violations
Generate a report artifact for each adapter run
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement submit validation and enqueue
userStories.description: As an operator, I want invalid runs rejected before queueing.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate required submit fields repo, base_branch, engine, auth_profile_id, and scope_ref
Reject profiles not in linked status and reject engine/provider mismatches
Enforce provider policy allow or deny before creating a queued run
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement atomic claim plus lock acquisition transaction
userStories.description: As a worker, I want ownership acquisition to be all-or-nothing.
acceptanceCriteria: Transition one eligible run to claimed and create an attempt row in one transaction
Acquire the auth lock lease in the same transaction
Roll back run claim and attempt creation when lock acquisition fails
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement heartbeat renewals
userStories.description: As a worker, I want lease renewals so healthy attempts are not reclaimed.
acceptanceCriteria: Update attempts.heartbeat_at and extend attempts.lease_expires_at on heartbeat
Update auth_profile_locks.heartbeat_at and extend auth_profile_locks.lease_expires_at on heartbeat
Emit a lease_lost terminal event and stop worker execution when lease renewal fails
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement stale-attempt reconciler
userStories.description: As an operator, I want stale attempts detected and closed automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Scan active attempts where lease_expires_at is earlier than current time
Mark stale attempts terminal with error_code stale_attempt
Release stale auth lock leases
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement retry scheduler with backoff
userStories.description: As an operator, I want deterministic retry transitions for retryable failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Support configurable max_attempts and exponential backoff
Transition retry-eligible runs through failed to retrying to queued
Keep runs beyond max_attempts in terminal failed state
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement hard-timeout enforcement
userStories.description: As an operator, I want overdue runs terminated automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Set deadline_at during submit using default or policy timeout
Mark overdue runs as failed with error_code run_timeout
Emit a run_timeout event on timeout transition
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Enforce cancellation propagation and claim exclusion
userStories.description: As an operator, I want cancellation intent enforced by workers and scheduler logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Check cancel intent in the worker loop on each heartbeat interval and stop active execution when set
Exclude terminal canceled runs from claim queries
Skip runs marked canceled in retry scheduling
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Enforce revoked-profile renewal denial
userStories.description: As an operator, I want revoked profiles unusable by active workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Fail lock renew with profile_revoked when auth profile status is revoked
Stop worker execution and release lock on profile_revoked
Cover revoke-during-run behavior with an integration test
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Add Daytona runner API client abstraction in Go
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a runner interface so Go services are decoupled from Daytona SDK language constraints.
acceptanceCriteria: Define Go interface methods CreateSandbox, Exec, StreamLogs, DestroySandbox, and Health
Use the interface in worker sandbox lifecycle code with no direct Daytona SDK imports
Send X-Service-Token on every runner client request
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement sandbox provisioning

Run hal init before restoring uploaded .hal state

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Hal execution runner

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement auth materialization with least-privilege permissions

Create auth directories with mode 0700 and files with mode 0600

userStories.title: [Backend] Integrate redaction into persistence and streaming pipelines

userStories.title: [Backend] Define .hal state bundle manifest
